reasoning: Bitcoin's current price as of October 26, 2025, is approximately $113,800, having recovered slightly from a major flash crash on October 10-11, 2025. During that crash, BTC fell from all-time highs above $125,000 to lows between $102,000 and $108,000, with some exchanges wicking down to $100,000 (though there is no definitive evidence it broke the $100k mark on Binance). There are just over two months left in the year. A drop to $99,999 requires roughly a 12% decline from current levels. Given Bitcoin's historical volatility—and the fact that 20-30% drawdowns from all-time highs are standard during bull markets—a retest of the recent lows that wicks below the psychological $100,000 level is highly plausible. Several analysts, including Standard Chartered, have already noted the potential for a drop below $100k. Given the time remaining and the proximity of the target price to recent lows, the likelihood of a Binance 1-minute candle wicking below $100,000 before the end of 2025 is slightly better than a coin toss.
